value.found_quote: The Bash sandbox lets Claude run most shell commands without stopping to ask permission. Instead of approving each command, you define which files and network domains commands can touch, and the operating system enforces that boundary for every Bash command and its child processes.
value.rationale: The page explicitly documents the "sandboxed Bash tool" as a built-in feature of Claude Code. It confirms OS-level filesystem and network isolation enforced for every Bash command and their child processes. The page describes it as generally available on macOS, Linux, and WSL2, with no preview/beta indicators. This directly substantiates all elements of the claim: it is built-in, per-command, provides OS-level filesystem and network isolation, and applies to every Bash command Claude runs and their child processes.
